steps.title: Spot the temporal endpoint
steps.content: The passage moves from “decades” of unsuccessful theories (“none had sufficient support”) directly to “scientists found strong evidence.” This marks the moment when the long search finally succeeds.

steps.title: Select the conclusion marker
steps.content:
Finally marks the moment when a prolonged effort ends in success—the word used to signal that long searching has yielded results.

The other choices either frame the finding as another failed theory, imply false causation, or wrongly group it with failures.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
